what_it_does: Headless conversation circuit for a character voice pipeline: receives a finished utterance as audio bytes, transcribes it with the configured STT provider, sends the transcript to the Company brain through the bridge, optionally synthesizes the reply via a selected TTS engine, and exposes VAD-based barge-in and utterance-endpoint hooks for a browser-side audio stream to drive.
observations: Runs in a separate Python 3.12 voice venv and reaches the brain via HTTP to the bridge instead of importing the 3.14 Suite, avoiding a forked brain/store/event log.
Derives TTS engine ports at import time from ops/services.json and fails loud if the registry is missing, malformed, or incomplete.
Reads the active STT provider from the bridge's /api/rhm-config slot, falling back to voice_stt.STT_DEFAULT or active_ear only when the bridge is unreachable.
The brain step is injectable: loop_turn accepts a think_fn so the bridge can pass the in-process Suite.chat, while standalone use falls back to HTTP /api/chat.
speak POSTs a selected engine's /tts endpoint and returns wav bytes; the voice argument semantics differ per engine family.
_voice_arg_for maps a persona's voice fields to whatever the selected engine expects, allowing any persona to be voiced through any engine.
select_persona loads a character sketch into the singleton brain by POSTing to /api/rhm-config.
loop_turn includes a speak_reply voice gate that skips synthesis for text-only modes, returning empty wav and null engine/voice.
voice_speakable.speakable cleans markdown/code/URLs from the reply before synthesis while the returned reply stays raw for the UI.
utterance_ended and barge_in are caller-driven VAD hooks that operate on a float32 mono 16k buffer.
standouts: Dual-mode brain access: the same module runs standalone over HTTP or embedded in the bridge with an injected in-process think_fn, preserving one brain instance.
Registry-is-truth design: ENGINE_PORTS is a live projection of ops/services.json rather than a hardcoded map.
Per-mode voice gate (speak_reply) prevents unnecessary TTS engine startup for text-only presences.
Persona-to-engine voice mapper decouples character design from TTS engine choice.
conventions: Fail-loud RuntimeError/ValueError with explicit, descriptive messages instead of silent defaults.
Environment-variable overrides for bridge URL, per-engine URL, and VAD silence window.
Injectable endpoints/callables rather than hardwiring UI or audio capture.
Single source of truth for engine ports (ops/services.json) and STT selection (bridge /api/rhm-config).
Heavy docstrings that explain design rationale alongside type hints.
concerns: _load_engine_ports performs file I/O and JSON parsing at module import time, so a missing or malformed ops/services.json crashes import.
default_ear catches Exception broadly and silently falls back, which could hide bridge config drift.
_post uses a single 120-second timeout for all brain/TTS calls with no retry, backoff, or cancellation mechanism.
No streaming or chunked audio support: speak returns a complete wav byte buffer.
VAD helpers assume voice_stt exports vad_speech_timestamps and vad_has_speech without an import-time guard.
utterance_ended calls semantic_complete() with no arguments, while its docstring describes semantic_complete(text)->bool, a contract mismatch.
notes: This is the headless voice turn machine: it wires STT, brain, and TTS through injectable hooks and HTTP endpoints, while leaving mic/speaker handling to the browser.
questions: What is the exact schema returned by /api/rhm-config and /api/chat, including optional fields like action and mode?
What does voice_speakable.speakable return or raise on bad input, and how does it handle each engine's expression tags?
Are vad_speech_timestamps and vad_has_speech guaranteed present in voice_stt, or are they optional extras?
How do the qwen3tts and cosyvoice /tts endpoints interpret a natural-language voice description string?
What persona fields are required beyond engine, brain, voices, voice, and voice_description?
Is there a plan for streaming TTS audio chunks, or is full wav synthesis the intended design?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly a headless conversation loop with injectable steps, HTTP brain access, and VAD hooks, and the implementation follows that shape.
intention_signals: Build a reusable, testable, headless voice-pipeline kernel that can run either standalone in the voice venv or embedded in the bridge, while keeping the brain singleton and letting operators swap STT/TTS engines and personas live.
novelty: The dual-mode brain injection (HTTP fallback versus in-process think_fn) in a single module, plus the persona-to-engine voice-argument mapper that decouples character design from TTS engine choice.
contribution: Provides the core shape of a voice turn (listen-think-speak) with explicit injection points for each step, a registry-derived engine port map, a per-mode voice gate, and VAD hooks that a browser audio stream can call.
summary_for_embedding: Headless STT-to-brain-to-TTS conversation loop with injectable steps, HTTP bridge brain access, registry-derived TTS engine ports, persona voice mapping, and VAD-based barge-in/turn-endpoint hooks.
intention_for_embedding: To implement a reusable voice-pipeline kernel that avoids forking the Company brain and lets the UI/audio layer drive turn detection and playback control.

symbols.does: Reads TTS engine ports from ops/services.json at import time.
symbols.description: Parses the services registry, extracts every tts-* entry's port, and returns an {engine: port} map. Raises RuntimeError loudly if the registry is missing, malformed, or lacks ports.

symbols.does: Builds the base URL for a named TTS engine.
symbols.description: Looks up the engine in ENGINE_PORTS and returns either an environment override or a localhost URL with the registry port. Raises ValueError for unknown engines.

symbols.does: POSTs JSON or raw bytes to a URL and returns parsed JSON or raw bytes.
symbols.description: Serializes the payload, sets the appropriate Content-Type, performs an HTTP POST with urllib, and returns either the response body as bytes or parsed JSON. Propagates HTTP and parse errors.

symbols.does: Determines the active STT provider from the bridge config.
symbols.description: Fetches /api/rhm-config from the bridge and returns its stt slot; if the bridge is unreachable or has no slot, falls back to voice_stt.STT_DEFAULT or active_ear. Keeps STT selection in one place.

symbols.does: Transcribes audio bytes using the selected STT provider.
symbols.description: Resolves the provider via default_ear or the caller override and calls voice_stt.transcribe. Returns the transcript dict and fails loud if the chosen ear is unavailable.

symbols.does: Sends a transcript to the Company brain through the bridge.
symbols.description: POSTs {message, graph_id, focus} to /api/chat and returns the reply dict. Used when no in-process think_fn is injected, ensuring the 3.12 voice process does not import the 3.14 Suite.

symbols.does: Synthesizes text to wav bytes via a named TTS engine.
symbols.description: POSTs {text, voice?, speed?} to the engine's /tts endpoint and returns raw wav bytes. Wraps connection errors in a descriptive RuntimeError naming the engine and URL.

symbols.does: Maps a persona record to the correct voice argument for a selected engine.
symbols.description: Returns a natural-language description for qwen3tts/cosyvoice, a named bank voice for orpheus/kokoro, or None for xtts/chatterbox so the engine uses its reference clip. Enables any persona to be voiced by any engine.

symbols.does: Loads a character persona into the brain via the bridge.
symbols.description: Retrieves the persona record and POSTs its brain sketch to /api/rhm-config so the singleton brain holds the character context. Returns the persona and resulting config.

symbols.does: Runs one full listen-think-speak voice turn for a character.
symbols.description: Transcribes the finished audio, optionally calls an injected think_fn or the HTTP think, optionally synthesizes the reply through the persona's engine (or overrides), and returns transcript, raw reply, engine/voice, wav bytes, and action/mode. Skips synthesis when speak_reply is False.

symbols.does: Detects whether the operator's current utterance has finished.
symbols.description: Uses VAD speech timestamps to check trailing silence; if a semantic_complete hook is provided, requires both silence and the hook to return True. Designed to be called by the browser audio stream.

symbols.does: Detects whether the operator started speaking during character playback.
symbols.description: Returns True if VAD finds speech in the provided mic buffer, signaling the UI to stop playback and start a new turn.
